title: Aspirin and Extended-Release Dipyridamole: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:
gnant, plan to become pregnant; or are breast-feeding. Aspirin may harm the fetus and cause problems with delivery if it is taken around 20 weeks or later during pregnancy. Do not take aspirin and extended-release dipyridamole around or after 20 weeks of pregnancy, unless you are told to do so by your doctor. If you become pregnant while taking aspirin and extended-release dipyridamole, call your doctor. if you are having surgery, including dental surgery, tell the doctor or dentist that you are taking aspirin and extended-release dipyridamole. Your doctor may tell you to stop taking aspirin and extended-release dipyridamole before surgery. talk to your doctor about the risks and benefits of taking aspirin and dipyridamole if you are 65 years of age or older. Older adults should not usually take aspirin and dipyridamole because it is not as safe or effective as other medications that can be used to treat the same condition. What special dietary instructions should I follow? 
Unless your doctor tells you otherwise, continue your normal diet while taking aspirin and extended-release dipyridamole.
What should I do if I forget a dose? 
Take the missed dose as soon as you remember it. However, if it is almost time for the next dose, skip the missed dose and continue your regular dosing schedule. Do not take a double dose to make up for a missed one.
